raw:
{
  "judge_id": "J001",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.1", "R1.2", "R1.4", "R1.6", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {
    "R1.1": 5,
    "R1.2": 5,
    "R1.4": 5,
    "R1.6": 3,
    "RX.1": 5,
    "RX.3": 5,
    "RX.4": 5,
    "RX.5": 5
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The agent identifies the core conceptual issue of the contradiction between 'lock-free' claims and mutex-based implementation, and proposes a reframing that aligns with prior work.",
    "R1.2": "The agent walks through the argument chain end-to-end, flagging every inconsistency (e.g., progress guarantees, memory fences) and proposing minimal repairs.",
    "R1.4": "The agent maps against multiple prior works (Kogan & Petrank 2011, Michael–Scott queue) and clearly articulates the delta between this work and existing literature.",
    "R1.6": "The agent improves several problem paragraphs by providing two coherent revisions that address the identified issues, though it does not perform a full copy-edit pass.",
    "RX.1": "The agent flags uncertainty for multiple claims (e.g., whether the system is mutex-based or lock-free) and provides clear conditions under which each revision would be appropriate.",
    "RX.3": "The agent stays within the agreed scope, addressing all critical issues and providing two coherent revisions without drifting from the task.",
    "RX.4": "The agent identifies harmful patterns in the original (e.g., false claims about progress guarantees) and avoids introducing new errors or fabrications.",
    "RX.5": "The agent traces every non-cosmetic change to specific issues in the original text, providing clear reasons for each revision."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.1": 0.9,
    "R1.2": 0.9,
    "R1.4": 0.9,
    "R1.6": 0.8,
    "RX.1": 0.9,
    "RX.3": 0.9,
    "RX.4": 0.9,
    "RX.5": 0.9
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.1: The agent identifies the core conceptual issue of the contradiction between 'lock-free' claims and mutex-based implementation, and proposes a reframing that aligns with prior work.
rationale.R1.2: The agent walks through the argument chain end-to-end, flagging every inconsistency (e.g., progress guarantees, memory fences) and proposing minimal repairs.
rationale.R1.4: The agent maps against multiple prior works (Kogan & Petrank 2011, Michael–Scott queue) and clearly articulates the delta between this work and existing literature.
rationale.R1.6: The agent improves several problem paragraphs by providing two coherent revisions that address the identified issues, though it does not perform a full copy-edit pass.
rationale.RX.1: The agent flags uncertainty for multiple claims (e.g., whether the system is mutex-based or lock-free) and provides clear conditions under which each revision would be appropriate.
rationale.RX.3: The agent stays within the agreed scope, addressing all critical issues and providing two coherent revisions without drifting from the task.
rationale.RX.4: The agent identifies harmful patterns in the original (e.g., false claims about progress guarantees) and avoids introducing new errors or fabrications.
rationale.RX.5: The agent traces every non-cosmetic change to specific issues in the original text, providing clear reasons for each revision.
